question: Design a simple logging system using polymorphism. Create a base `Logger` class with a virtual `log(string message)` method. Derive `ConsoleLogger` and `FileLogger` classes, overriding `log()` to print to console or write to a file, respectively.

test_cases.input:
Application initialized successfully.
User 'admin' logged in.
Data backup started.
Data backup completed with 100 files.

expected.console_output:
[INFO] [Console] Application initialized successfully.
[INFO] [Console] User 'admin' logged in.
[INFO] [Console] Data backup started.
[INFO] [Console] Data backup completed with 100 files.
expected.file_content:
--- Log Session Started ---
[INFO] [File] Application initialized successfully.
[INFO] [File] User 'admin' logged in.
[INFO] [File] Data backup started.
[INFO] [File] Data backup completed with 100 files.
--- Log Session Ended ---

test_cases.input:
New session started.
Task X completed.

expected.console_output:
[INFO] [Console] New session started.
[INFO] [Console] Task X completed.
expected.file_content:
--- Log Session Started ---
[INFO] [File] New session started.
[INFO] [File] Task X completed.
--- Log Session Ended ---
